purpose: Concept-level dimensional knowledge for architecture planning. These are practical planning ranges, not final statutory/code-certified dimensions.

unitPolicy.rule: When user gives feet, keep feet. When technical detail is needed, include mm equivalent.

safetyBoundary: Do not claim these are final legal/code dimensions.
Always say final dimensions must be checked against local bylaws, NBC/BIS, fire rules, accessibility rules, and professional drawings.
Use these as concept-planning defaults for AI planning and image/drawing prompt generation.

notes: Entry buffer before living is preferred when space allows.
Keep near main entrance.
Avoid using living room as only long passage.
Useful when guest zone is separate from family lounge.
Should connect to kitchen.
Can combine with living in small homes.
Ventilation/exhaust required.
Utility/wash nearby preferred.
Place near kitchen/washing zone.
Private zone preferred.
Attached toilet and wardrobe wall should fit.
Window/ventilation should be planned.
Avoid direct kitchen opening.
Study/storage clearance should be considered.
Can be near common toilet.
Good daylight and quiet zone preferred.
Northeast/east/north preferred when practical.
Accessible from lobby/living/dining, not through bedroom.
Align with shaft/wet wall where possible.
Useful near living/dining for guests.
Can connect bedroom to attached toilet.
Road/open-side balcony gives better value.
Exact stair depends on riser/tread/floor height.
Independent stair useful for rental/future floors.
Lift dimensions must be finalized with lift vendor and code/accessibility requirement.
Front/road-side placement common.
Entry gate turning should be checked.
Can fit in front/side setback depending on local rules.

corridorPassage.publicBuilding: 5 ft or more concept depending on occupancy
notes: Public/commercial corridor must be checked against fire/accessibility codes.
Final clear height depends on local code, HVAC, beams, false ceiling, and building type.

mainDoor.premium: 4 ft or double shutter as design
notes: Main entry should feel clear and visible.
Furniture movement should be considered.
Accessible toilets require separate checks.
Fire/accessibility/egress rules must be checked.

windows.bedroomConcept: 4 x 4 ft or as facade/light permits
notes: Window size depends on light, ventilation, elevation, privacy, and code.

furnitureClearances.kitchenPassage: 3.5 to 4 ft preferred for working aisle
furnitureClearances.carDoorClearance: 2.5 to 3 ft side clearance preferred if space allows

residentialStair.widthConcept: 3 to 3.5 ft minimum practical; 4 ft comfortable
residentialStair.warning: Final stair dimensions must follow applicable code and building type.

publicStair.widthConcept: larger than residential; depends on occupancy/fire egress
publicStair.warning: Public/commercial stair sizing must be code/fire-consultant checked.

notes: Ramp needs landing, handrail, slip-resistant surface, and local accessibility compliance check.

retailShop.showroom: frontage and depth based on display
notes: Front display/customer movement and storage needed.
Circulation, meeting, pantry, storage, and services extra.

restaurantDining.conceptPerSeat: 12 to 18 sq ft including circulation concept
notes: Kitchen, wash, store, toilet, service aisle extra.
Exhaust, fire, grease, drainage, washable surfaces required.
Privacy, handwash, patient chair, examination couch clearance.
Chair clearance, compressor/services, sterilization support.

classroom.concept: area depends on student count and education norms
notes: Daylight, ventilation, board visibility, exits, toilets, fire safety.

warehouseBay.concept: module based on rack size, vehicle movement, loading bay, clear height
notes: Structure and fire design must be specialized.

workshop.concept: equipment footprint plus safe working clearance
notes: Power, ventilation, fire, material movement needed.

plumbingShaft.multiStorey: size based on fixtures and maintenance access
notes: Final shaft size by plumbing design.

electricalPanelZone.residentialDB: small accessible wall zone away from wet areas
electricalPanelZone.commercialPanelRoom: dedicated accessible service space depending load
notes: Final sizing by electrical load calculation.
Drain, access, noise, and heat rejection must be considered.

toiletExhaust.concept: external wall, duct, or shaft route required
notes: Closed toilet without exhaust/shaft is bad planning.

waterTankPump.concept: terrace/underground/pump room space depends on occupancy
notes: Final capacity and structural support require professional design.
